harry_takes: Anthropic's $6B February revenue puts them on unicorn pace; Mythos leak shows massive capabilities being held back
OpenAI shutting down Sora is admission their consumer strategy was fundamentally flawed
AI agents will leak data 100x faster than humans; security incidents are becoming the price of speed
OpenAI's internal chaos is strategic mistake; Anthropic's consistency gives it execution advantage
harry_boldest_quote: We may be at the stage where we throw the humans under the bus, not the AI anymore, which I think at some level is pretty terrifying.
predictions: Lemkin: OpenAI must hit $20-70B ARR in consumer ads to remain competitive; single-digit millions today means existential pressure
O'Driscoll: Sora kill proves strategy was wrong; this is self-inflicted damage from prioritizing consumer experience over compute efficiency
All: Autonomous 24/7 agents running on Anthropic models will accelerate security incidents and data leaks as unintended side effect
disagreements: Harry suggests OpenAI's consumer strategy can still work with focus; Lemkin argues Sora kill proves the bet was catastrophic waste and priority misalignment.
harry_role: Balanced critic. Credits OpenAI for admitting Sora was mistake. Defends 100M ads revenue as early-stage. Worries OpenAI's chaos is permanent, suggests Brett Taylor as day-to-day CEO. Mostly on side of Anthropic's consistency.
notable: Mythos model leak has cybersecurity stocks tank despite model being designed for security—peak irony. Also marks Anthropic as willing to hold back capabilities, suggesting massive hidden roadmap.
